data.task_instructions: Read the loaded paper and extract exactly one allele or variant candidate: the Drosophila Notch facet-glossy allele, written as Nfa-g, N^{fa-g}, or similar. Preserve the paper notation, genotype/phenotype context, organism hints, and one record_evidence verified quote. Do not resolve allele IDs in the extractor.

run_finished.response:
The extraction from the paper identified the Drosophila Notch facet-glossy allele as "Nfa-g." Here are the details:

- **Allele Notation**: Nfa-g
- **Genotype/Phenotype Context**: The allele is mentioned in the context of a genetic construct: "Nfa-g; P[w+]36.1/Act5C>y+>Gal4 UAS-GFP; UAS- NCD/ hsFLP MKRS."
- **Organism Hint**: Drosophila
- **Record Evidence Verified Quote**: "Nfa-g; P[w+]36.1/Act5C>y+>Gal4 UAS-GFP; UAS- NCD/ hsFLP MKRS (Fig. 3E)"
- **Section**: Methods, subsection "2. Clonal analyses," page 1.

This completes the task of extracting the specified allele from the document.

evidence_records.verified_quote: Nfa-g; P[w+]36.1/Act5C>y+>Gal4 UAS-GFP; UAS- NCD/ hsFLP MKRS (Fig. 3E)

toolArgs.claimed_quote: in Nfa-g mutants, rst F6 -lacZ was expanded to all pigment cells surrounding cone cells (Fig. 4C-C0), indicating that Notch is necessary for suppressing rst in emerging PPCs.

details.friendlyName: Allele/Variant Extraction Agent: Record Evidence complete

toolArgs.claimed_quote: In the N^{fa-g} mutant, the rst transcript was detected in cells adjacent to cone cells (open arrowheads).
